summary: Azure resource groups — the container every other Azure resource in a subscription lives in. Provisioning a new resource group is engineer-reviewed; tag updates are guardrailed here because resource-group tags often carry estate-wide governance and cost-allocation meaning.

operations.title: Provision a new resource group
operations.summary: Request a brand-new resource group; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new resource group — the container every other Azure resource in this subscription is created inside. Submitting routes the naming and region choice to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish; nothing is applied without review.

params.name: rg_name
params.label: Resource group name
params.help: Letters, numbers, underscores, parentheses, hyphens and periods, 2-90 characters, must not end with a period. Names the container in Azure and in the generated draft.

params.name: location
params.label: Region
params.help: The Azure region this resource group and everything created inside it lives in. The engineer picks the region during review — it is a data-residency and architecture decision this form does not make for the operator.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Resource group name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this resource group holds
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this resource group.

decisions: Region choice is a data-residency and architecture decision — confirmed by the engineer before applying
Naming should follow the account's resource-group naming convention

operations.title: Update tags on a resource group
operations.summary: Set or update this resource group's tags. Many teams rely on them for governance and cost reporting, so this stays guardrailed.
operations.description: Sets or merges tags on the resource group via the `tags` map. Resource-group tags are often relied on for cost allocation and governance reporting across every resource inside the group, so the change is guardrailed even though it is non-disruptive and reversible.

params.name: resource_group
params.label: Resource group
params.help: The resource group to tag, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed resource group can be targeted.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Key/value tags to set; existing keys not listed here are left untouched.
